title: Air accident monthly bulletin November 2025
body: "November Bulletin 11/2025   AAIB Field Investigations  A field investigation is an independent investigation in which AAIB investigators collect, record and analyse evidence.   The process may include, attending the scene of the accident or serious incident; interviewing witnesses; reviewing documents, procedures and practices; examining aircraft wreckage or components; and analysing recorded data.   The investigation, which can take a number of months to complete, will conclude with a published report.  Commercial Air Transport - Fixed wing  Boeing 737-8K5 (G-TAWB), a member of Cabin Crew fell from steps whilst closing the aircraft door for departure, East Midlands Airport, 16 December 2024   General Aviation - Fixed wing  SportStar SLM (G-CMGB), loss of control shortly after taking off in conditions of reduced visibility, Chesterfield, Derbyshire, 1 September 2024    AAIB Correspondence Investigations   These are reports on accidents and incidents which were not subject to a Field Investigation.   They are wholly, or largely, based on information provided by the aircraft commander in an Aircraft Accident Report Form (AARF) and in some cases additional information from other sources.   The accuracy of the information provided cannot be assured.   Commercial Air Transport  ATR 72-212 A (G-IACZ), aircraft diverted because the flaps could not be fully extended on approach to Cornwall Airport, Newquay, 15 April 2025 Boeing 737-86N (G-NPTA), loss of cabin pressurisation, approaching Edinburgh Airport, 17 May 2025   Miscellaneous  This section contains Addenda, Corrections and a list of the ten most recent Air Accident reports published by the AAIB.   Bulletin Addendum: Rans RV-8A, G-RVBJ, Bolthead Airfield, Devon, 20 June 2024   Record-Only Investigations  This section provides details of accidents and incidents which were not subject to a Field or full Correspondence Investigation.  They are wholly, or largely, based on information provided by the aircraft commander at the time of reporting and in some cases additional information from other sources.  The accuracy of the information provided cannot be assured.  Record-only Correspondence investigations reviewed in August - September 2025.  "
